matter.title: Petition for Guardianship of a Minor by Olivia B. Ellsworth
matter.summary: Olivia B. Ellsworth, the adult sibling of one minor child(ren) (Marcus B. Kingsley), petitions the Bath Probate Court for full guardianship of a minor because of a parent's substance use treatment, and asks the court to authorize the petitioner to make day-to-day, school, and medical decisions for the child(ren).

fact_pattern.summary: Olivia B. Ellsworth, the adult sibling of one minor child(ren) (Marcus B. Kingsley), petitions the Bath Probate Court for full guardianship of a minor because of a parent's substance use treatment, and asks the court to authorize the petitioner to make day-to-day, school, and medical decisions for the child(ren).
fact_pattern.narrative:
Olivia B. Ellsworth is the adult sibling of one minor child(ren): Marcus B. Kingsley. Because of a parent's substance use treatment, the child(ren)'s parent or parents are temporarily unable to provide full-time care, and Olivia has stepped in to ensure the child(ren) are safe, supported, and able to stay in their routine.

At present, the child splits time between relatives. Without a guardianship order, Olivia lacks clear legal authority to enroll the child(ren) in school activities, consent to medical care, and handle the ordinary decisions of daily life. Olivia seeks full guardianship of a minor to provide that authority and a stable home.

Olivia now petitions the Bath Probate Court under the Maine Probate Code for appointment as guardian of the minor child(ren), to remain in effect until the minor reaches 18 or further court order. The petition will be served on the child(ren)'s parents and other interested persons as the statute requires, and Olivia asks the court to find that the appointment is in the child(ren)'s best interests.
undisputed_facts: Olivia B. Ellsworth is the adult sibling of one minor child(ren).
The child(ren)'s parent or parents are, for the time being, unable to provide full-time care because of a parent's substance use treatment.
Olivia B. Ellsworth is seeking appointment as guardian of the minor child(ren) in Sagadahoc County Probate Court.
disputed_facts: Whether the statutory basis for appointing a guardian of the minor is satisfied, including consent of the parents or a clear-and-convincing showing in the absence of consent.
Whether all parents and interested persons have received the notice the statute requires.
Whether the proposed guardianship, and its scope and duration, serves the best interests of the child(ren).

timeline.event: Petition for guardianship of a minor prepared for filing in Bath Probate Court.

third_parties.name: Desmond S. Noe
third_parties.relevance: Oversees the child's routine medical care and can speak to ongoing health needs and the authority required to address them.

third_parties.name: Lillian K. Danforth
third_parties.relevance: Holds parental rights; her consent to or objection to the guardianship is central, and she is entitled to notice.

exchanges.question: How are you related to the child or children, and how long have you been involved in their lives?
exchanges.answer: I am the adult sibling. I have been closely involved with the child(ren) for a long time, and lately I have been the one making sure they are cared for day to day.

exchanges.question: What has changed that makes a guardianship necessary right now?
exchanges.answer: Because of a parent's substance use treatment, the parent or parents cannot provide full-time care at the moment. I want to make sure the child(ren) stay safe and settled while that is worked out.

exchanges.question: Do the parents support this, or do you expect any objection?
exchanges.answer: I have talked with the family about it. The court will need to know whether the parents consent or object, and I want the parents to receive proper notice either way so this is done correctly.

exchanges.question: Where are the children living now, and how are they doing?
exchanges.answer: Right now, the child splits time between relatives. They are doing as well as can be expected, and my goal is to keep their home, school, and routine as steady as possible.

exchanges.question: What specifically do you need to be able to do for the children?
exchanges.answer: I need clear authority to handle school enrollment and activities and to consent to medical care and appointments. Without an order, people keep telling me they cannot deal with me because I am not the legal guardian.

goals: Obtain legal authority to make school and medical decisions for the minor child(ren).
Provide the child(ren) with a safe, stable home for as long as the parents are unable to do so.
Ensure all parents and interested persons receive proper notice so any resulting order is sound.
priorities: The safety and best interests of the child(ren).
Minimizing disruption to the child(ren)'s home, school, and routine.
A clear, enforceable order that lets the petitioner act for the child(ren).
desired_outcomes: Appointment of Olivia as guardian with authority over the child(ren)'s school and medical decisions.
An order, effective until the minor reaches 18 or further court order, that keeps the child(ren) in a stable placement.
non_starters: Any arrangement that endangers the child(ren) or compromises their safety.

constraints.budget: Modest; the petitioner wants an efficient, low-conflict path to a guardianship order.
constraints.timeline: Wants authority in place promptly so school and medical decisions are not delayed.

issues.issue: Hearing and findings required for appointment
sub_questions: What findings must the court make to appoint a guardian?
What scope and limits should the letters of guardianship include?
issues.client_position: The record supports the findings needed for appointment with authority over the child(ren)'s care, schooling, and medical decisions.

issues.issue: Notice to parents and other interested persons
sub_questions: Who must be served with the petition and notice of hearing?
Has notice been given in the manner and time the statute requires?
issues.client_position: The petitioner will provide notice to the parents and all interested persons as required before any hearing.

issues.issue: Best interests of the child as the governing standard
sub_questions: How does the proposed guardianship serve the child(ren)'s best interests?
Does it preserve stability and continuity for the child(ren)?
issues.client_position: Appointing the petitioner as guardian is in the child(ren)'s best interests and keeps them safe and settled.

issues.issue: Parental consent versus the clear-and-convincing showing required without it
sub_questions: Have the parents consented to the guardianship?
If a parent objects, can the petitioner show by clear and convincing evidence that appointment is warranted?
issues.client_position: Where the parents consent, the petitioner relies on that consent; where they do not, the petitioner is prepared to make the heightened showing the statute requires.

issues.issue: Statutory basis for appointing a guardian of a minor
sub_questions: Are the parents unable or unavailable to provide care so that a guardian is needed?
Do the facts support appointment of the petitioner as guardian?
issues.client_position: The petitioner is an appropriate guardian and the statutory grounds for appointment are met.

evidence.title: Proposed guardian's background-check results
evidence.description: Background screening of the proposed guardian relevant to suitability.

evidence.title: Child's medical records
evidence.description: Records reflecting the child's ongoing health needs and care.

evidence.title: Signed parental consent / affidavit
evidence.description: Sworn consent of a parent to the guardianship, where consent is given.

amounts.label: Child's estimated monthly expenses
amounts.basis: Estimated monthly cost of the child's food, clothing, school, and activities.

amounts.label: Survivor / Social Security benefits received for the child
amounts.basis: Monthly benefits, if any, received on the child's behalf.

facts.current_living_arrangement: the child splits time between relatives
facts.duration: until the minor reaches 18 or further court order
